conflicts.description: Sadie's daughters are introduced as Lena Kincaid and Bess Holt in chapter 1, but the hearing chapter names the disputants Hester and Pearl.
conflicts.canonical_value: Sadie's claimants are Hester (Kincaid/Harmon line) and Pearl (Dunn line).
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 4 embeds Hester and Pearl more deeply in the hearing's causal logic, claim ruling, and later references, so those names are treated as canonical and the chapter 1 pair is logged as a continuity slip.

conflicts.description: Gabriel's copied ledger memory says the missing premolar was upper right, but the exhumation and hearing both identify the missing tooth on the upper left.
conflicts.canonical_value: Gabriel is identifiable by a missing upper left premolar.
conflicts.resolution_note: The public exhumation and sworn courtroom testimony both place the tooth on the left and drive the legal outcome, so they outrank the single earlier ledger-memory line.

conflicts.description: Gabriel's burial is described as east of the broken cedar, then as south of the cedar root under washed-away child markers, then later in court as north side six rows from the cedar stump.
conflicts.canonical_value: Gabriel's grave is kept at the more stable level of detail: the lower fever plot beside the broken cedar landmark.
conflicts.resolution_note: The earlier recognition and the physical exhumation agree on the broken-cedar landmark, while the courtroom north-side phrasing appears to be a later paraphrase conflict. The sheet therefore retains the general lower-fever-plot location rather than contradictory micro-directions.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet collapses the ledger theft and the affidavit reveal into chapter 08, but the manuscript steals the ledger in chapter 8 and reveals the active Eli affidavit in chapter 9.
conflicts.canonical_value: Ledger theft belongs to chapter 08; Lucille's revelation of the Eli affidavit and ten-day contest window belongs to chapter 09.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript separates the theft from the county paperwork reveal by one chapter, so the updated timeline splits them instead of compressing them.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet compresses several late-act beats into the wrong chapters: the paternity confession is placed with Gabriel's exhumation, and Silas's death and the reservoir fill are placed too early.
conflicts.canonical_value: Gabriel is identified in chapter 13; Della confesses the paternity truth in chapter 14; Noreen gains standing in chapter 15; the final move and Silas's death happen in chapter 16; the reservoir overtakes the old road in chapter 18.
conflicts.resolution_note: Each beat changes what later scenes can do, so the updated timeline restores the manuscript's chapter-by-chapter sequence instead of the earlier compressed summary.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet implies Rafe's false affidavit is simply destroyed in chapter 14, but the manuscript keeps a withdrawn county record and only burns Rafe's marked paper copy.
conflicts.canonical_value: The county retains a withdrawn affidavit record, while Rafe destroys only his paper copy in chapter 14.
conflicts.resolution_note: The hearing still turns on the filed and withdrawn record, so the object status has to distinguish the surviving county paperwork from the burned household copy.
